expect: genesis_extract.py produces <500KB bundle. 3-layer design from L-1471. Daughter orient.py runs from extracted state.
actual: Tool built. Minimal bundle 579KB (14 core tools). orient.py fails — full dep tree is 46 modules / 642KB. Without tools: 425KB. Three viable tiers: passive (425KB, no orient), core (579KB, no orient), full (1.1MB, orient works). orient dep tree IS the reproductive cost.
diff: Expected <500KB with working orient: FALSIFIED. orient.py dep tree (46 modules) was underestimated by L-1467 ('8.6% = 11 core deps'). 11 is import count, not transitive closure. Key finding: orient.py transitive dependency tree is 46/128 tools (36%), not 11/128 (8.6%). Reproduction has 3 tiers, not 1.
method: Built genesis_extract.py with 4-layer extraction (identity, orientation, hub_lessons, core_tools). BFS on orient.py import graph to compute transitive closure. Extracted to /tmp/daughter-swarm and tested orient.py execution at each tier.
key_finding: Orient.py transitive dependency tree is 36% of all tools (46/128), not 8.6% (11/128). L-1467 measured direct imports, not transitive closure. This means reproduction has 3 viable tiers: passive daughter (identity+orientation+hubs, 425KB), core daughter (+ 14 core tools, 579KB), full daughter (+ 46 orient deps, 1.1MB). The orient dep tree is the single largest reproductive cost.
verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. genesis_extract.py built and functional. Bundle sizes correct. orient.py dependency gap reveals 3-tier reproduction model not previously identified.
